client_request: We need a booking system with appointments, reminders and admin management.

projectOverview.clientDescription: We need a booking system with appointments, reminders and admin management.
projectOverview.cleanDescription: Development of a comprehensive web-based system designed to facilitate online appointment booking, automate customer reminders, and provide robust administrative tools for managing services, resources, and user accounts. The system aims to streamline operations, enhance user experience, and reduce no-show rates.
projectOverview.businessGoal: To streamline the appointment booking process for customers, reduce no-shows through automated reminders, enhance operational efficiency for service providers and administrators, and improve overall resource utilization.

objectives: Enable customers to easily browse services and book, view, modify, or cancel appointments online 24/7.
Automate the delivery of appointment reminders to customers via email and/or SMS to reduce no-show rates by at least 15%.
Provide administrators and service providers with intuitive tools to manage services, staff availability, schedules, and customer information efficiently.
Improve data accuracy and reporting capabilities related to appointment bookings, cancellations, and resource utilization for informed business decisions.

stakeholders.description: Individuals or entities who will use the system to book, view, and manage their appointments for various services.
Personnel within the organization who provide the services and need to view their schedules, manage appointments assigned to them, and update their availability.
Key personnel responsible for configuring system settings, managing services, resources, user accounts, roles, and accessing reports and analytics.
Stakeholders responsible for the overall business strategy, ensuring the system meets organizational goals, and overseeing system adoption and performance.

functionalRequirements.title: Customer Appointment Booking
functionalRequirements.description: The system shall allow customers to browse available services, view real-time availability of time slots, and book new appointments. This includes selecting a service, preferred date/time, and any specific service provider if applicable.
acceptance_criteria: A customer can successfully select a service and an available time slot.
A customer receives an immediate confirmation of their booked appointment.
The system prevents double-booking of time slots or resources.

functionalRequirements.title: Appointment Management (Customer & Staff)
functionalRequirements.description: The system shall provide functionality for customers to view their upcoming and past appointments, and to reschedule or cancel existing appointments. Service providers shall be able to view, update, and manage all appointments relevant to their schedule or assigned services.
acceptance_criteria: A customer can view a list of all their current and past appointments.
A customer can successfully reschedule an appointment to an available slot.
A service provider can mark an appointment as 'completed' or 'no-show'.

functionalRequirements.title: Automated Reminder Notifications
functionalRequirements.description: The system shall automatically send reminders to customers for upcoming appointments via email and/or SMS. Reminders should be configurable by administrators regarding timing (e.g., 24 hours, 1 hour before).
acceptance_criteria: Customers receive an email reminder at the configured interval before their appointment.
Customers receive an SMS reminder (if opted-in) at the configured interval.
Administrators can configure the timing and content of reminder notifications.

functionalRequirements.title: Service & Resource Configuration
functionalRequirements.description: Administrators shall be able to define and manage the services offered (e.g., service name, duration, price), associated resources (e.g., rooms, equipment), and the availability of individual service providers.
acceptance_criteria: An administrator can create a new service with all required attributes.
An administrator can set the working hours and unavailability periods for each service provider.
Changes to service or provider availability are immediately reflected in the customer booking interface.

functionalRequirements.title: User Authentication & Management
functionalRequirements.description: The system shall provide secure user registration and login functionalities for customers, service providers, and administrators. It must also allow administrators to manage user accounts, assign roles, and reset passwords.
acceptance_criteria: New customers can register for an account and log in securely.
Administrators can create, edit, disable, and delete staff and administrator accounts.
Role-based access ensures users can only access features permitted by their assigned role.

functionalRequirements.title: Reporting & Analytics
functionalRequirements.description: The system shall provide administrators with access to reports and dashboards showcasing key metrics such as total bookings, cancellations, no-show rates, and service provider utilization over specified periods.
acceptance_criteria: An administrator can generate a report on monthly booking trends.
The system can display the no-show rate for specific services or providers.
Reports are exportable in common formats (e.g., CSV, PDF).

nonFunctionalRequirements.title: System Availability
nonFunctionalRequirements.description: The system shall be available for use 99.9% of the time, excluding scheduled maintenance windows, during operational hours (24/7).

nonFunctionalRequirements.title: Data Security & Privacy
nonFunctionalRequirements.description: The system shall protect all sensitive customer and organizational data using industry-standard encryption protocols (in transit and at rest). Access to administrative functions and sensitive data must be strictly controlled by a robust role-based access control (RBAC) mechanism. The system must comply with relevant data privacy regulations.

nonFunctionalRequirements.title: Performance & Response Time
nonFunctionalRequirements.description: Key user interactions (e.g., loading a booking calendar, submitting an appointment) shall complete within 3 seconds under normal load (up to 100 concurrent users).

nonFunctionalRequirements.title: Usability & User Experience
nonFunctionalRequirements.description: The user interface for all stakeholders (customers, service providers, administrators) shall be intuitive, easy to navigate, and require minimal training for basic operations. The design should be responsive, adapting well to various screen sizes (desktop, tablet, mobile).

nonFunctionalRequirements.title: Scalability
nonFunctionalRequirements.description: The system shall be capable of supporting an increase in user base and booking volume of up to 50% year-over-year for the next three years without significant performance degradation or requiring a complete architectural redesign.

assumptions: The client will provide all necessary service details, staff information, and initial configuration data prior to system setup.
A reliable internet connection is available to all users (customers, staff, administrators) accessing the web-based system.
The client will select and provide access to a third-party email and/or SMS gateway for sending automated reminders.
Standard web browser compatibility (latest two versions of Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari) is sufficient, and no specific legacy browser support is required.
The initial scope focuses on online appointment booking; complex payment processing or POS integration is not a primary requirement for the first phase.
constraints: The project must adhere to a strict development timeline of 6 months from project kickoff to initial production deployment.
The solution must be a purely web-based application; a dedicated native mobile application is not within the current scope or budget.
Integration with existing internal systems (if any) is not part of the current scope, unless explicitly specified.
The project budget is capped at an amount to be determined, which will dictate specific technology choices and scope prioritization.
acceptanceCriteria: All identified critical and high-priority functional requirements are implemented, thoroughly tested, and perform as expected.
The system successfully meets defined non-functional requirements, especially regarding security, performance, and availability.
Key stakeholders (customers, service providers, administrators) are able to perform their core tasks using the system efficiently and without significant issues.
The system is successfully deployed to a stable production environment and is accessible to end-users.
